title: Federal Law--Civil Law Harmonization Act, No. 1
title_en: Federal Law--Civil Law Harmonization Act, No. 1
description: Federal Law—Civil Law Harmonization Act, No. 1 (Federal Law--Civil Law Harmonization ...). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1 This Act may be cited as the Federal Law—Civil Law Harmonization Act, No. 1 .

provisions.title: Title
provisions.content: 2 This Part may be cited as the Federal Law and Civil Law of the Province of Quebec Act .

provisions.title: Provisions repealed
provisions.content: 3 (1) The provisions of the Civil Code of Lower Canada , adopted by chapter 41 of the Acts of 1865 of the legislature of the Province of Canada, entitled An Act respecting the Civil Code of Lower Canada , are repealed in so far as they relate to subjects that fall within the legislative competence of Parliament and have not been expressly repealed. (2) The Interpretation Act applies to the repeal referred to in subsection (1).

provisions.title: Substitution
provisions.content: 4 Sections 5 to 7, which apply solely in the Province of Quebec, are to be interpreted as though they formed part of the Civil Code of Québec .

provisions.title: Consent required
provisions.content: 5 Marriage requires the free and enlightened consent of two persons to be the spouse of each other. Previous Version

provisions.title: Minimum age
provisions.content: 6 No person who is under the age of sixteen years may contract marriage.

provisions.title: Monogamy
provisions.content: 7 No person may contract a new marriage until every previous marriage has been dissolved by death or by divorce or declared null.

provisions.chapter: PART 2 Amendments to the Interpretation Act
provisions.title: Monogamy

provisions.content: 177 (1) The definition secured creditor in subsection 2(1) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act , as enacted by section 25 of this Act, applies only to bankruptcies or proposals in respect of which proceedings are commenced after the coming into force of that section, but nothing in this subsection shall be construed as changing the status of any person who was a secured creditor in respect of a bankruptcy or a proposal in respect of which proceedings were commenced before the coming into force of that section. (2) Paragraph 136(1)(e) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act , as enacted by section 31 of this Act, applies only to bankruptcies or proposals in respect of which proceedings are commenced after the coming into force of that section, but nothing in this subsection shall be construed as changing the status of any person who was a secured creditor in respect of a bankruptcy or a proposal in respect of which proceedings were commenced before the coming into force of that section. (3) Paragraph 178(1)(d) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act , as enacted by section 32 of this Act, applies only to bankruptcies in respect of which proceedings are commenced after the coming into force of that section.

provisions.title: Coming into force
provisions.content: 178 The provisions of this Act, other than Part 8, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
